steps.title: Identify the relationship
steps.content: The sentence contrasts two time periods: in 2005 LaMontagne performed more than thirty live sets, while in 2004 he had performed fewer than a dozen times. The blank must signal that the second clause opposes the first.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — often,: an adverb, not a transition word — placing it here produces an incoherent construction and signals no logical relationship. ✗
  • Choice B — additionally,: signals addition, implying the two clauses support the same idea — but the clauses contrast, not supplement each other. ✗
  • Choice C — on the other hand,: signals contrast or opposition, which fits exactly: 30+ sets in 2005 vs. fewer than a dozen in 2004. ✓
  • Choice D — similarly,: signals likeness — but a jump from fewer than twelve to more than thirty is not similar; it is the opposite. ✗
That is the correct answer.
